commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the direct-helper fail-closed compatibility behavior, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff adds no dependency, workflow, credential, package-resolution, or supply-chain surface and reduces a media response memory-exhaustion risk. commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 16 MiB default response cap for Google Chat media downloads when callers omit `maxBytes`, and adds a regression test for that path.

PR surface: Source -4, Tests +36. Total +32 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current `downloadGoogleChatMedia` can call `fetchBuffer` with `maxBytes` omitted, and that successful response path buffers via `arrayBuffer()` without a cap; this read-only review did not run tests.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the direct-helper fail-closed compatibility behavior, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(googlechat): media downloads can grow unbounded without a size cap
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where direct callers of the Google Chat media download API could omit `maxBytes` and let a successful media response buffer through an uncapped `arrayBuffer()` call. The normal inbound attachment path already passes a configured cap, but the exported helper still had an uncapped fallback.

## Why This Change Was Made

Google Chat media downloads now use a 16 MiB default response cap whenever callers do not provide a stricter cap. Existing callers that pass `maxBytes` keep their configured limit and error text.

The fallback is fixed at the helper boundary because omitted `maxBytes` means the direct caller did not opt into channel media configuration. Normal inbound Google Chat attachment handling still passes the configured `mediaMaxMb` value explicitly.

## User Impact

Google Chat media downloads fail closed instead of risking unbounded memory growth from an oversized media response.

## Evidence

- Red test before the fix: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts -- --run` failed because the no-`maxBytes` path called `arrayBuffer()` (`unbounded arrayBuffer should not be called`).
- After fix: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts -- --run` -> 1 file / 25 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts` -> clean.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts` -> clean.
- Copied terminal output from loopback streamed media proof: a local HTTP server streamed a 32 MiB response through the same bounded reader with the 16 MiB Google Chat overflow error:

```text
$ node_modules/.bin/tsx ../../outputs/candidate-googlechat-bound-api/googlechat-media-loopback-proof.mjs
overflow_error=Google Chat media exceeds max bytes (16777216)
max_bytes=16777216
total_bytes_available=33554432
bytes_sent_before_cancel=17825792
full_response_buffered=no
```

AI-assisted: built with Codex
target.author: sunlit-deng
